mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the Fed Funds rate, prompted by high inflation, signal aggressive monetary tightening. This increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC statements from March 2022 signaled the start of a hiking cycle.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a >0.20pp monthly FEDFUNDS increase during high inflation periods (CPI YoY > 4%).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and aggregate demand. This translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% in March 2022 reflects a tight labor market.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a rank information coefficient below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation growth outpaces nominal retail sales growth, real consumer spending is contracting. This erosion of real demand leads to lower corporate revenue expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month % change in CPIAUCSL > 3-month % change in RSAFS
evidence_refs: fact:CPI inflation is at 40-year highs in early 2022.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in over 50% of periods where the 3-month growth rate of CPIAUCSL exceeds that of RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production indicates expanding output and economic capacity utilization. This signals stronger corporate earnings across the industrial sector and drives positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The 6-month momentum of INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate from its recent low is a leading indicator of a recession. This triggers expectations of falling corporate profits and causes a broad-based sell-off in equities.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns, indicating the signal is not a reliable predictor of negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an upside surprise in monthly CPI inflation increases the probability of more aggressive future rate hikes by the central bank, leading to an immediate negative repricing of equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest CPI reading of 287.708 is a multi-decade high.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following CPI releases > 0.6% is not statistically different from zero during periods when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity. As a key leading indicator for the business cycle, this negatively impacts forward-looking equity market expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive sector.
failure_condition: The 3-month momentum of HOUST has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not consistently negative (i.e., correlation > -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is actively raising interest rates, strong real GDP growth figures are perceived negatively by the market, as they signal the economy can withstand further tightening, increasing the expected terminal policy rate.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has begun its hiking cycle as of March 2022.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles (rising FEDFUNDS), S&P 500 20-day returns following strong GDP prints (>2.5%) are not statistically lower than returns following weak GDP prints (<2.5%).
